methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Pre-Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $12.0B total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Infrastructure & Logistics (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Durin is a pre-seed infrastructure and logistics company founded by Ted Feldmann that has raised $3.4M to date. The comparable universe for this stage and sector is limited: two private peers (AiDash and Watoga Technologies) exist in Infrastructure & Logistics, though neither meets the criteria for same-stage or adjacent-stage peer matching. No public comparables were identified in the narrow peer set.

A stage-progression methodology is applicable here. Using the $3.4M raised as a proxy anchor and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from pre-seed to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $30.0B. This approach assumes Durin will progress through venture funding milestones at rates consistent with frontier-infrastructure companies, though this extrapolation carries material risk at the pre-seed stage when execution and market validation remain unproven.

The peer-median approach was not applicable due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables in the sector. A broader sector median would conflate disparate business models and scales, introducing noise rather than signal.

The triangulated valuation range is $25.5B to $34.5B (median $30.0B), derived from a single applicable method. This narrow methodological basis and the lack of directly comparable pre-seed peers represent the analysis's primary limitations. The valuation is highly sensitive to assumptions about company progression velocity and ultimate market opportunity realization.
